data.body:
[G4 child-consistency — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S1 6d56: Faithful, lock-free snapshot materialization core)]

Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this materialization-core child against the WHOLE sibling roster (S2 70c7 cache+janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing, S6 1ba5 docs). All 7 modes are clean under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. EMPTY G4 findings list for this pairing.

Mode-by-mode:

1. Implicit shared state — CLEAN. The values this child produces feed a consistent producer→consumer chain with single named owners: the resolved immutable SHA + the snapshot dir (produced by materialize(); consumed by S2's cache keying via "populated via S1's atomic dir-rename", by S3's gate re-rooting, and ultimately pinned/signed as verified_at_sha by S4). The atomic-population primitive (build into tmp/<uuid>/, fsync, rename into the cache path) is OWNED here and CONSUMED by S2 (its What: "populated via S1's atomic dir-rename"; its Scope: "Out: materialization itself (S1)") — a clean producer/consumer split, no double-implementation of population. The temp-root/flock portability seam (gettempdir/mkdtemp, fcntl.flock + atomic-mkdir fallback, REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR) is established here and merely documented by S6 (its AC5) and ridden by S2's cross-process locks/<sha> + gc/lock. No shared-state value is produced without a consumer or consumed without this producer.

2. Conflicting assumptions — CLEAN (examined the S1↔S3 local-mode seam closely). This child's materialize() signature takes source_mode and its error bullet says "attested mode fails closed, local mode still runs"; S3 routes local mode to an in-place checkout read ("no materialization, no cache"). Under the benign reading these are consistent, not contradictory: S1's "local mode still runs" describes fail-soft fetch behavior (a fetch/auth failure must not abort the local verify-before-push path), which is exactly what S3's in-place-read local mode needs; attested fails closed (S1 AC5) matches S4's signing path requiring a resolved SHA. No high-confidence contradiction. Faithfulness assumptions also agree across the roster: S1's "faithful read-tree+checkout-index, NOT git archive" underpins S4's reproducibility claim (AC1 "verdict over identical content") and the parent's AC1 "faithful snapshot."

3. Dependency gap — CLEAN at this child. Every obligation this child delegates points to a present, claiming sibling: the cache/janitor that consumes its atomic population (S2 70c7), gate wiring (S3 fa49), signing (S4 30b3), surfaces (S5 b7fa) — all enumerated in this child's Scope "Out" and all real roster members. The descriptive credential MESSAGE detail is cross-ref'd to S6 ("see S6"), claimed by S6 AC1/AC2. NOTE: the standing roster dependency-gap finding — the pinned-SHA snapshot handle not routed into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/freshness re-check — is a seam between S3 and S4 (downstream consumers of this child's snapshot handle); it does NOT originate at this materialization-core child and is out of this pairing's scope. This child correctly produces the resolved-SHA snapshot dir; WHO threads it into attest.py is an S3/S4 seam, already filed on 30b3.

4. Scope overlap — CLEAN. The atomic dir-rename population sits at the S1↔S2 boundary but is cleanly split: S1 OWNS the population primitive; S2 CONSUMES it for cache entry creation and adds eviction (rename-to-trash) on top — no double-implementation (S2 Scope: "Out: materialization itself (S1)"; S1 Scope: "Out: the cache/janitor (S2)"). The flock/portability seam is OWNED here (the materialize-side flock+mkdir fallback) while S2's cross-process locks/<sha> + gc/lock RIDE the same seam without re-owning the tar/tmp/fallback sub-clauses. The descriptive fetch/auth error is RAISED here (its AC5) and DOCUMENTED by S6 (its AC2) — raiser/documenter split, no overlap.

5. Ordering violation — CLEAN. The encoded dependency graph (S1 → {S2, S3}; S3 → {S4, S5}; S4,S5 → S6) makes this child the ROOT producer, which is coherent with the producer→consumer flow: materialization precedes caching/threading/signing/surfacing/docs. The edges, where present, point the benign way; no ordering contradiction. Parent AC8 only requires all-closed + green.

6. Consumer impact — CLEAN. This child's contract (materialize(ref, *, source_mode) -> context-managed handle; resolved immutable SHA; faithful snapshot dir; descriptive fetch errors) is consumed without redefinition by S2 (caches the handle), S3 (re-roots gate file tools onto the snapshot dir, and its AC6 tolerates an absent/being-GC'd dir via ENOENT→re-materialize), S4 (signs over the resolved SHA), S5 (surfaces verified_at_sha + surface-level validation around the fetch errors this child raises). No downstream contract is broken by this child's interface; the ENOENT-miss tolerance S3 promises is consistent with the delete-on-last-close reader model S2 builds atop this child's population.

7. Residual references — CLEAN. All cross-refs resolve: S6 (descriptive credential message detail), S2/S3/S4/S5 (the "Out" delegations), REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR (this child introduces it; S6 documents it), the uploadpack.allowReachableSHA1InWant / --filter=blob:none fetch-prerequisite notes (operational prose, no unresolved sibling dependency), and the EFS/NFS flock caveat (tracked on alto-fruit-punch per the parent's Out-of-scope). The LFS-pointer-detection and submodule-handling obligations (this child's AC2) are owned here, not dangling. No dangling reference.

Conclusion: no high-confidence cross-child inconsistency between this materialization-core child (S1) and the parent/siblings. Its atomic-population, faithful-extraction, portability-seam, fetch-coalescing, and descriptive-error deliverables form clean producer/consumer splits with S2 (population→cache), S3 (snapshot→gate re-rooting), S4 (SHA→signing), S5 (surfacing), and S6 (docs). The two standing roster seams (S3↔S4 attest.py snapshot-handle routing; S3↔S5 code-review base/head surface retirement) live on other children and do not touch this pairing. Empty G4 findings list for this pairing.
